allies.rationale_text: Confirmation bias amplifies WYSIATI — not only does available evidence feel complete, but the search for more evidence is also biased toward confirming the story already constructed; WYSIATI closes the picture while confirmation bias reinforces it.
allies.source_quote: Confirmation bias amplifies WYSIATI: not only does the available evidence feel complete, but the search for more evidence is also biased toward confirming the story already constructed.

allies.rationale_text: The first piece of evidence encountered anchors the subsequent story-building that WYSIATI performs — the anchor is not just a reference point, it is the seed from which the entire coherent narrative grows.
allies.source_quote: The first piece of evidence encountered anchors the subsequent story-building that WYSIATI performs.

allies.rationale_text: Survivorship bias produces the structured missing denominator that WYSIATI then fails to register — the survivors are in the picture, the failures are absent, and WYSIATI reports the picture as complete.
allies.source_quote: Survivorship bias produces the structured missing denominator that WYSIATI then fails to register.

antagonists.rationale_text: Base rates provide the outside-view evidence that WYSIATI excludes — the distributional facts about the reference class that are not in the immediate picture.
antagonists.source_quote: Base rates provide the outside-view evidence that WYSIATI excludes: the distributional facts about the reference class not in the immediate picture.

antagonists.rationale_text: Critical thinking's discipline of evidence quality assessment and assumption surfacing directly fights WYSIATI by inserting the 'what is missing?' question before conclusions are drawn.
antagonists.source_quote: Critical thinking fights WYSIATI by inserting the 'what is missing?' question before conclusions are drawn.

structured_tensions.tension_text: WYSIATI conflicts with base-rates when the mind's closure around available evidence prevents the outside-view reference-class data from entering the picture at all.
structured_tensions.source_quote: WYSIATI vs base-rates: WYSIATI conflicts with base-rates (base rates) when the mind's closure around available evidence prevents the outside-view reference-class data from entering the picture at all.

structured_tensions.tension_text: WYSIATI conflicts with information-asymmetry when the mind treats currently-available information as complete, suppressing the recognition that the other party has material information absent from the current picture.
structured_tensions.source_quote: WYSIATI vs information-asymmetry: WYSIATI conflicts with information-asymmetry (information asymmetry) when the mind treats currently-available information as complete.

structured_tensions.tension_text: WYSIATI conflicts with probabilistic-thinking when narrative coherence generates overconfidence in conclusions drawn from incomplete evidence, collapsing what should be a distribution into a single confident point estimate.
structured_tensions.source_quote: WYSIATI vs probabilistic-thinking: WYSIATI conflicts with probabilistic-thinking (probabilistic thinking) when narrative coherence generates overconfidence in conclusions drawn from incomplete evidence.

structured_tensions.tension_text: WYSIATI conflicts with critical-thinking when the fluency and internal coherence of the available story suppresses the signal that scrutiny, gap-identification, and assumption-testing are needed.
structured_tensions.source_quote: WYSIATI vs critical-thinking: WYSIATI conflicts with critical-thinking (critical thinking) when the fluency and internal coherence of the available story suppresses the signal that scrutiny is needed.

curation_notes.summary: WYSIATI Wave 3 curated from the new source markdown. Key allies: confirmation-bias (amplifies closure), anchoring (seeds the narrative), survivorship-bias (produces the structured denominator gap). Key antagonists: base-rates (imports outside-view), critical-thinking (forces gap audit). Four structured tensions.
